meta.title: EMPiRE — seizure risk in pregnancy (women with epilepsy)
meta.authors: Allotey J, Fernandez-Felix BM, Zamora J, et al.
meta.outcome: Probability of at least one epileptic seizure during pregnancy (to 6 weeks postpartum) in a woman with epilepsy, assessed at the antenatal booking visit
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The EMPiRE model (Allotey et al., PLOS Medicine 2019) estimates the probability of at least one seizure during pregnancy (through 6 weeks postpartum) in a woman with epilepsy, from factors known at the antenatal booking visit. Developed on 399 and externally validated on 128 pregnancies (C-statistic 0.79/0.76).

How to read it

  • Strongest drivers: a tonic-clonic seizure in the 3 months before pregnancy, non-tonic-clonic seizure type, higher lamotrigine dose, and a learning difficulty / mental-health disorder. Older age at first seizure is protective.

Applies to women on lamotrigine, carbamazepine, phenytoin or levetiracetam. Demonstration tool for antenatal counselling.

predictors.name: Tonic–clonic seizure in the 3 months before pregnancy
predictors.assess: Any tonic–clonic (convulsive) seizure in the 3 months before pregnancy. Strongest factor (OR 7.2).

predictors.name: Non-tonic–clonic seizure in the 3 months before pregnancy
predictors.assess: Any non-tonic–clonic seizure in the 3 months before pregnancy (OR 1.94).

predictors.name: Baseline seizure classification
predictors.assess: Predominant seizure type at the booking visit (tonic–clonic = reference).

predictors.name: Learning difficulty or mental-health disorder
predictors.assess: A learning difficulty or mental-health disorder (OR 1.96).

predictors.name: Admitted to hospital for seizures in a previous pregnancy
predictors.assess: Hospital admission for seizures in a previous pregnancy (OR 1.19).

predictors.name: Age at first seizure
predictors.assess: Age (years) at the first-ever seizure. Older onset is protective (OR 0.98 per year).

predictors.name: Lamotrigine dose
predictors.assess: Baseline lamotrigine dose (mg/day); 0 if not taking it. Higher dose = higher risk (OR 1.34 per 100 mg).

predictors.name: Levetiracetam dose
predictors.assess: Baseline levetiracetam dose (mg/day); 0 if not taking it (OR 1.02 per 100 mg).

model.panel_title: Chance of a seizure during pregnancy
model.note: Outcome = ≥1 epileptic seizure from the antenatal booking visit through 6 weeks postpartum. Enter ASM doses in mg/day (0 if not taking). Only lamotrigine and levetiracetam carry dose terms in the model.
model.viz_info: Each factor adds its published log-odds to a logistic model; the sum plus the intercept gives the probability of a seizure during pregnancy.

provenance.source: Allotey 2019 PLOS Med — published logistic equation (intercept -1.39 + 9 terms) + Table 2 ORs — doi 10.1371/journal.pmed.1002802, PMID 31083654
provenance.notes: EMPiRE logistic, intercept -1.39. β: age-first-seizure -0.02/yr; classification non-TC 0.75/unspecified 0.61 (ref TC); TC-in-3mo-pre-preg 1.97; nonTC-in-3mo 0.66; LTG 0.29/100mg (0.0029/mg); LEV 0.02/100mg (0.0002/mg); learning/mental 0.67; prev-pregnancy-admission 0.17. Verified worked example (age25,nonTC,LEV3000,LTG400,nonTC-pre,prev-adm)→81% (nomogram 80%). C 0.79/0.76. Built 2026-07-08.
